employment_history.reason_for_leaving: Currently employed; considering new opportunities for growth, a shorter commute, and a role with more responsibility in practice management and staff development.
employment_history.duties_description_1: Oversee day-to-day operations of a busy community health clinic, including front desk workflows, patient scheduling, insurance verification, and coordination between medical assistants, nurses, and providers.
employment_history.duties_description_2: Supervise a team of 7 administrative staff members, handle hiring and training, manage the appointment system (Epic), monitor patient flow and wait times, develop and update office procedures, assist with billing questions, respond to patient concerns, and coordinate with outside labs and imaging centers.
employment_history.reason_for_leaving_2: Accepted a promotion to Medical Office Administrator at Harborview Community Health Center in Seattle to gain leadership experience and work in a larger, more complex clinic environment.
employment_history.duties_description_1_2: Managed front desk operations including greeting patients, answering multi-line phones, scheduling appointments, verifying insurance, and collecting copays and outstanding balances.
employment_history.duties_description_2_2: Prepared and updated patient charts in the electronic health record (Athenahealth), processed referrals and prior authorizations, coordinated with pharmacies for prescription refills, and helped train new reception staff on office procedures and customer service expectations.
employment_history.reason_for_leaving_3: Left to take a full-time role with daytime hours and benefits at Soundview Family Practice; the urgent care role was primarily evenings and weekends, which became difficult to balance with family commitments.
employment_history.duties_description_1_3: Checked in urgent care patients, confirmed identification and insurance, collected copays, and entered visit reasons accurately and quickly to support triage in a fast-paced setting.
employment_history.duties_description_2_3: Coordinated patient flow with nursing staff, maintained a calm waiting room environment during high-volume periods, handled basic medical record requests, and assisted with end-of-day cash reconciliation and daily reporting.

training_and_certification.subjects_of_special_study_or_research_wo: Completed a capstone research project in my Medical Office Administration certificate program on improving patient check-in efficiency and reducing no-show rates in community health clinics. I analyzed three years of de-identified appointment data from a local clinic, compared reminder methods (phone, SMS, email), and recommended a combination of automated text reminders and same-day confirmation calls, which reduced no-shows in the pilot group by approximately 18%.

Independently studied HIPAA compliance and patient privacy best practices, focusing on real-world breach case studies. I created my own reference guide summarizing practical do’s and don’ts for front-desk staff (for example, how loudly to call patients’ names, what information can be left on a voicemail, and how to handle requests from family members who are not listed on a patient’s release form). I shared this guide with my team at Harborview, and parts of it were incorporated into our new-hire onboarding materials.
training_and_certification.special_training_or_qualifications_descr: Completed a Medical Office Administration Certificate (North Seattle College, 2019) with coursework in medical terminology, healthcare billing and coding basics (ICD-10, CPT), electronic health records, and customer service in healthcare settings. I also completed a 160-hour externship in a family medicine clinic where I learned hands-on front desk workflows, appointment triage, and basic insurance verification. In addition, I am fully vaccinated (including annual flu shots and COVID-19 boosters) and familiar with maintaining up-to-date employee health records required in clinical settings.

Extensive on-the-job training in two major EHR systems (Epic and Athenahealth), including building and managing provider schedules, running daily and monthly operational reports, documenting patient messages accurately, and using in-basket workflows. I have also received internal training on conflict de-escalation and trauma-informed communication, which has helped me handle difficult situations such as upset patients receiving unexpected bills, walk-ins turned away due to capacity limits, and family members anxious about wait times. I am fluent in English and conversational in Spanish, which I regularly use to help Spanish-speaking patients with basic check-in instructions and form completion, while bringing in a certified interpreter for more complex medical discussions.
training_and_certification.activities_civic_athletic_etc: Volunteer patient navigator at the Seattle Community Free Clinic two evenings per month, where I help patients complete intake forms, explain clinic policies in clear and simple language, and coordinate with volunteer providers to keep the visit flow moving. I have also helped organize annual flu shot clinics at my current job, including putting up flyers, answering staff questions, and assisting with check-in on the day of the event. Outside of healthcare, I participate in a local recreational soccer league on Sunday mornings and occasionally help coach my niece’s youth team when her coach needs an extra adult. I also enjoy organizing small neighborhood events, such as a yearly block potluck, where I manage sign-up sheets, communicate logistics, and make sure everyone feels welcomed and included.
